description_long: This is a weathered, pole-mounted traffic light featuring two vertically stacked signal housings, each containing three circular lenses. The upper housing displays the standard red, yellow, and green lights, while the lower housing also contains three lenses, likely for additional signaling or directional control. The pole is metallic and shows signs of rust and grime, anchored to a robust concrete or metal base for stability. The signal housings are rectangular with rounded edges and have protective visors over each lens to shield from sunlight and rain. The entire structure is utilitarian, designed for durability in outdoor urban environments, and the visible wiring and mounting brackets suggest easy maintenance access. The surface finish is matte and rough, with visible dirt and wear indicating long-term exposure to the elements.
description: A weathered, pole-mounted traffic light with two stacked signal housings, each containing three colored lenses, mounted on a sturdy base and showing signs of outdoor wear.
description_view_0: Front view showing both signal housings with all six lenses (red, yellow, green) clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the protruding visors over each lens and the depth of the signal housings, as well as the mounting brackets and wiring.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the backs of the signal housings, the pole, and the mounting structure, with no visible lenses.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, showing the visors and the layered structure of the signal housings.
